stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Lina Iris Viktor is a British-Liberian conceptual artist.
  • She is based in London and in New Orleans, Louisiana.
  • One of Viktor’s paintings features dancer Akua Naru.
  • In the painting, Naru is standing in profile.
  • Three gilded sculptures are arranged on the floor in front of her.
stem: The student wants to describe where Naru is in the painting to an audience already familiar with Viktor and Naru. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Akua Naru is in a painting by Lina Iris Viktor, a conceptual artist based in London and New Orleans, Louisiana.
In Viktor’s painting, Naru is standing in profile, with three gilded sculptures arranged on the floor in front of her.
Three gilded sculptures appear in the foreground of Lina Iris Viktor’s painting of dancer Akua Naru.
Lina Iris Viktor is a conceptual artist based in both London and New Orleans, Louisiana.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. It describes Naru’s location in Viktor’s painting (standing in profile) and omits redundant background information about artists already familiar to the audience.

Choice A introduces Viktor as if to an unfamiliar audience. Choice C describes the foreground rather than Naru’s position. Choice D identifies Viktor and her locations without describing Naru’s position.
